learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher explained ㅂ irregular conjugation with cold weather

examples.english: It's so cold because it's winter.
It's cold outside, so please wear a coat.

learning_materials.notes: ㅂ irregular verb - ㅂ drops when adding vowel endings

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher showed formal ending conjugation for ㅂ irregular

examples.english: Today's weather is cold.
The room is cold. Please turn on the heating.

learning_materials.notes: Formal polite ending for ㅂ irregular adjectives

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher explained how ㅂ changes to 워 with 요 ending

examples.english: My hands are so cold.
It's cold because of the air conditioner.

learning_materials.notes: ㅂ becomes 워 when adding 요 ending

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher showed how to modify nouns with cold

examples.english: The cold winter has come.
I want to drink a cold beverage.

learning_materials.notes: Used to modify nouns - 'cold weather', 'cold day', etc.

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher used as another example of ㅂ irregular

examples.english: It's really hot in summer.
The sauna is too hot.

learning_materials.notes: Another ㅂ irregular adjective like 춥다

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher showed ㅂ to 워 conjugation pattern

examples.english: Today's weather is hot.
I'm hot from exercising.

learning_materials.notes: ㅂ irregular conjugation - 덥다 becomes 더워요

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher explained noun modification with hot

examples.english: It's hot summer weather.
I ordered hot coffee.

learning_materials.notes: Modifies nouns - 'hot weather', 'hot food', etc.

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher used as example of ㅂ irregular conjugation

examples.english: This problem is easy.
Korean is not easy.

learning_materials.notes: ㅂ irregular adjective meaning easy

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher showed ㅂ to 워 pattern with easy

examples.english: Math is easy.
Cooking is easy.

learning_materials.notes: 쉽다 becomes 쉬워요 with ㅂ irregular rule

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher used as example of ㅂ irregular

examples.english: The exam was difficult.
This book is too difficult.

learning_materials.notes: ㅂ irregular adjective - opposite of 쉽다

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher demonstrated ㅂ irregular conjugation

examples.english: Korean pronunciation is difficult.
This game is difficult.

learning_materials.notes: 어렵다 becomes 어려워요 following ㅂ irregular pattern

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher used as food-related ㅂ irregular example

examples.english: Kimchi is spicy.
This ramen is too spicy.

learning_materials.notes: ㅂ irregular adjective for describing spicy food

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher showed spicy food conjugation

examples.english: Tteokbokki is spicy.
The pepper is spicy.

learning_materials.notes: 맵다 becomes 매워요 with ㅂ irregular conjugation

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher used as complex ㅂ irregular example

examples.english: The flowers are beautiful.
The scenery is beautiful.

learning_materials.notes: ㅂ irregular adjective - more formal than 예쁘다

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher explained complex ㅂ irregular conjugation

examples.english: This painting is beautiful.
The music is beautiful.

learning_materials.notes: 아름답다 becomes 아름다워요 - complex ㅂ irregular

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher explained honorific form of beautiful

examples.english: Your mother is beautiful.
The teacher is beautiful.

learning_materials.notes: Honorific form - 시 is added, ㅂ becomes 우

learning_materials.english: not only... but also, in addition to
learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher explained this emphasizing connector

examples.english: Not only is it raining, but it's also windy.
Not only do I have a lot of homework, but I also have an exam.

learning_materials.notes: Used for emphasis - adds additional information

learning_materials.context_from_class: Main grammar pattern taught - cause and effect realization

examples.english: While studying, I realized time passed quickly.
While exercising, I found myself getting healthier.

learning_materials.notes: Shows realization or discovery during an ongoing action

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher used as example of 다가 보니 pattern

examples.english: While walking home, I ran into a friend.

examples.korean: 공원을 걸어가다 보니 예쁜 카페를 발견했어요.
examples.english: While walking through the park, I discovered a pretty cafe.

learning_materials.notes: Common example of discovering something while walking

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher emphasized this time adverb with 다가 보니

examples.english: I keep forgetting.
The phone keeps ringing.

learning_materials.notes: Frequently used with 다가 보니 pattern

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher used with 다가 보니 pattern

examples.english: We meet often.
I exercise frequently.

learning_materials.notes: Similar to 자꾸 but less repetitive feeling

learning_materials.context_from_class: Example from textbook exercise

examples.english: While reading the novel, I ended up crying.
While reading the newspaper, I realized it got late.

learning_materials.notes: Common pattern with reading activities

learning_materials.context_from_class: Example from textbook showing conversation context

examples.english: While talking with my friend, I felt better.

examples.korean: 선생님과 얘기하다 보니 답을 알게 되었어요.
examples.english: While talking with the teacher, I came to know the answer.

learning_materials.notes: Shows discovery through conversation

learning_materials.context_from_class: Example about developing feelings through meeting

examples.english: While meeting my new colleague, we became close.

examples.korean: 이웃을 만나다 보니 좋은 사람인 걸 알았어요.
examples.english: While meeting my neighbor, I realized they're a good person.

learning_materials.notes: Often used for developing relationships

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher used as example with 다 보니 pattern

examples.english: I become lazy on weekends.
I'm a lazy student.

learning_materials.notes: Descriptive adjective for personality trait

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher showed adjective use with 다 보니

examples.english: Being lazy, I couldn't do my homework.
Being lazy, I woke up late.

learning_materials.notes: Shows how a state/trait leads to a result

learning_materials.english: to be scared easily, to be timid
learning_materials.context_from_class: Student used this to describe herself

examples.korean: 저는 겁이 많아서 혼자 못 가요.
examples.english: I'm timid so I can't go alone.
I'm a person who scares easily.

learning_materials.notes: Common way to describe being fearful or timid

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher explained indirect question formation

examples.english: Do you know when they're coming?
I don't know where they're going.

learning_materials.notes: Used with verbs for indirect questions

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher explained adjective form of indirect questions

examples.english: I'm curious whether the food is delicious.
I'm worried whether the exam will be difficult.

learning_materials.notes: Used with adjectives for indirect questions

learning_materials.english: do you know what it is
learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher's example of indirect question

examples.english: Do you know what this is?
I know what the problem is.

learning_materials.notes: Common indirect question pattern

learning_materials.english: do you know how much it is
learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher's example for price questions

examples.english: Do you know how much the bus ticket is?
I'm curious how much the house costs.

learning_materials.notes: Used for asking about prices indirectly

learning_materials.english: do you know if (someone) is going
learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher's example with verb

examples.english: Do you know if your friend is going to the party?
Please tell me when you're going.

learning_materials.notes: Indirect question with movement verb

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher's past tense example

examples.english: Please check if the child ate.
I don't remember if I took the medicine.

learning_materials.notes: Past tense indirect question

learning_materials.english: the question was resolved, mystery was solved
learning_materials.context_from_class: From reading passage about solving a mystery

examples.english: Finally the mystery was solved.
After hearing the explanation, my question was resolved.

learning_materials.notes: Used when understanding something that was confusing

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher explained this word from reading passage

examples.english: Who is the author of this book?
It's a work by a famous author.

learning_materials.notes: Formal word for author, from 짓다 (to write/compose)

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher compared with 지은이

examples.english: Do you have a favorite writer?
I read that writer's new novel.

learning_materials.notes: More commonly used than 지은이

learning_materials.context_from_class: From reading passage about temple visit

examples.english: Buy tickets at the ticket office.
Where is the ticket office?

learning_materials.notes: Place where tickets are sold

learning_materials.context_from_class: Temple mentioned in reading passage

examples.english: Have you ever been to Cheongpyeongsa?
Cheongpyeongsa is a famous temple.

learning_materials.notes: Specific temple name from the reading

learning_materials.context_from_class: City mentioned in reading passage

examples.english: I went to Chuncheon for fun.
Chuncheon is famous for dakgalbi.

learning_materials.notes: City in Korea, known for chicken ribs

learning_materials.context_from_class: From reading passage describing when the visit happened

examples.english: There are fewer people on weekdays.
Shall we meet on a weekday afternoon?

learning_materials.notes: Monday through Friday, working days

learning_materials.english: to make matters worse, adding insult to injury
learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher mentioned this idiom in relation to 는데다가

examples.english: I'm late and to make matters worse, it's raining too.
To make matters worse, I lost my wallet too.

learning_materials.notes: Four-character idiom meaning bad situation getting worse

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher's example of forgetting something at home

examples.english: I left my keys at home.
I left my book at the library.

learning_materials.notes: Common expression for forgetting to bring something

learning_materials.context_from_class: Example sentence from exercise

examples.english: Exercising every day became a habit.
Going to bed early became a habit.

learning_materials.notes: Used to describe forming good or bad habits

learning_materials.english: because I grew fond of, developed affection
learning_materials.context_from_class: Example about marriage reason from textbook

examples.korean: 이 동네에 정이 들어서 이사 안 갈 거예요.
examples.english: I've grown fond of this neighborhood so I won't move.
I grew fond of the puppy so I decided to raise it.

learning_materials.notes: Describes developing emotional attachment over time

learning_materials.english: fell in love at first sight
learning_materials.context_from_class: Question from textbook about marriage

examples.english: We got married after falling in love at first sight.
I fell in love with that dress at first sight and bought it.

learning_materials.notes: Can be used for people or things you immediately love

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher corrected student's use of 혹시

examples.english: It will probably rain tomorrow.
I'll probably be late.

correction_details.explanation: 혹시 means 'by any chance' for questions, 아마도 means 'probably' for statements

learning_materials.notes: Used for probability, not 혹시 which is for questions

learning_materials.context_from_class: Teacher corrected student's future tense form

examples.english: I will cancel the meeting.
I will cancel the reservation.

correction_details.explanation: Future tense needs ㄹ/을 before 거예요

learning_materials.notes: Future tense requires proper conjugation with 할
